hard.next_step: Pending I&O content - check on NAPES attendance; debrief on Pinnacle content with George Jannino (CPO) and Czarina Whitmore; share a new whitepaper beyond the repeated Hackett/Broadridge benchmark; longer term collaborate on Supplier Onboarding.
hard.ais_why: Intake and Orchestration framed as workflow/speed/transparency for casual end-users, not as Merlin agentic AI; next step is event invites and Hackett benchmark sharing with no AI product positioning.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / no-decision (buyer not ready for I&O)

north_star_verdict.headline: Nurture-mode Intake & Orchestration opp on an account that already rejected our CLM bid over a clunky, bolt-on AI experience; Czarina Whitmore said Raymond James is 'not ready for I & O' (9 Apr 2026) and no buyer has replied since, so this is a no-decision fight, not a competitive one.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-12-05, still Qualified after ~7 months with no progression to Formal Evaluation or Shortlisted. Close date pushed twice on 2026-06-17 (2026-06-30 to 2026-09-30 to 2027-01-15), now 193 days out, a placeholder not a buyer milestone. Last genuine two-way buyer exchange was Czarina Whitmore's 'not ready for I & O' email on 2026-04-09, ~88 days ago and past the 60-day cold line. Every touch since (Hackett reports, Pinnacle/NAPES/ISM/ProcureCon invites) is one-way rep outreach; LastActivityDate 2026-07-02 is rep-side, not a buyer reply.
evidence: Czarina Whitmore emailed 'We're not ready for I & O at this time' on 2026-04-09; no buyer reply logged in the ~88 days since (SF Task 2026-04-09 [Clari - Email Received]).
Prior CLM deal on this account was LOST; the debrief was that 'our UX/CX to leverage any of our AI was clunky and seemed like a bolt-on' (SF Task 2026-07-02).
George Jannino (SVP Chief Procurement Officer), the likely economic buyer, has never replied on this opp despite ISM, ProcureCon and Pinnacle invites (SF Tasks 2026-04-01, 2026-06-25).
Close date pushed twice on 2026-06-17 with no buyer-driven milestone (SF OpportunityFieldHistory).
Ten competitors carried on the record with no shortlist refinement and no active evaluation (SF Competitors__c).
No quantified business case, ROI, or compelling event on record; rep is still offering to build an RFFS business case (SF Task 2026-07-07).

metrics.narrative: No quantified value case exists for the Intake & Orchestration opportunity. Across Salesforce free-text fields and the activity trail the buyer has never stated a cycle-time, cost, headcount, or error-rate target, and there are zero transcribed calls this sweep to source one. The only economic thread is the rep repeatedly sharing the Hackett procurement benchmark (Next Step 2026-07-02), which the buyer treated as 'same as earlier benchmark report', so it did not create urgency. The next move is to co-build a Raymond James-specific RFFS (financial-services) intake business case, which the rep already flagged as needed on 2026-07-07, and get Czarina Whitmore to validate the numbers.
sources: SF Task 2026-07-07 (RFFS business case not yet built)

economic_buyer.narrative: George Jannino, SVP Chief Procurement Officer, is the clear economic buyer for a $400k procurement I&O purchase and is a named Salesforce contact, so the EB is mapped, but he is not engaged. Every touch to him is one-way: ISM invite (2026-04-01), a ProcureCon follow-up and lunch offer (2026-06-25), and a Pinnacle invite, with no reply on record. Czarina Whitmore (VP, Procurement Sourcing & Operations) is the working-level owner and the only contact who replies, but she does not control budget and told us they are not ready. The move is to convert one of George's events (Pinnacle debrief) into a direct working session rather than another invitation.

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria for Intake & Orchestration are documented. There is no RFP, RFI, use-case list, or workshop feedback on this opp, and Business_Requirements__c-type signal is absent from the activity trail. What the evidence DOES show is a negative criterion inherited from the lost CLM deal: the buyer found Zycus AI 'clunky' and 'a bolt-on' (Task 2026-07-02), so a native, non-bolt-on agentic experience is an implicit bar we must clear. The move is to surface I&O criteria directly with Czarina Whitmore and Alexander Guzman before proposing anything.

decision_process.narrative: There is no visible decision or evaluation process for I&O because the buyer has not started one, stating on 2026-04-09 they are 'not ready for I & O at this time'. The stakeholder trail (Czarina Whitmore working-level, George Jannino as CPO, Eduardo Gomez in IT Security for a later InfoSec gate) implies a normal procurement-led committee if an evaluation opens, but no dates, steps, or approval chain are on record. The move is to get Czarina to name the trigger and timeline that would make I&O a live priority, converting an open-ended nurture into a dated re-evaluation.
sources: SF Task 2026-04-09 ('not ready for I & O')

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in play because the deal is pre-evaluation; there is no MSA, SOW, Order Form, or legal/procurement motion, and Current_Contract_Expiration__c gives no forcing function. Eduardo Gomez (Information Technology Security Analyst) is on the contact roster, which signals an InfoSec review would gate any future purchase at Raymond James (a regulated BFSI buyer), but nothing has been initiated. Visibility will only come once an evaluation opens and a timeline exists.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Eduardo Gomez, IT Security Analyst)

identify_pain.narrative: No confirmed operational pain has been articulated by the buyer for Intake & Orchestration. The inferred pain is manual/decentralised intake and supplier-onboarding friction, implied by the rep's Supplier Onboarding collaboration thread and the product scope (Merlin Intake, iSupplier), but the buyer has not owned that pain out loud and in fact declined to progress. The one confirmed, buyer-owned data point is dissatisfaction with a bolt-on AI experience from the lost CLM evaluation. The pain owner would be Czarina Whitmore's procurement operations team; the move is a discovery session to make them state the intake pain in their own words.

champion.narrative: No champion is advocating for Zycus. Czarina Whitmore (VP, Procurement Sourcing & Operations) is the most engaged contact and does reply and accept updates ('I appreciate the update', 2026-04-09), which makes her a coach, but she declined to progress I&O and shares no internal intelligence or internal push, so advocacy is absent. George Jannino (CPO) and Alexander Guzman (Director, Strategic Sourcing & Procurement Operations) have not engaged. In a regulated BFSI account that just rejected our CLM bid, the deal cannot restart without one contact willing to sponsor I&O internally. The move is to earn Czarina or Alexander with a financial-services-specific proof point before any commercial step.

competition.narrative: The named competitive field carries ten vendors (Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, Oracle, SAP Ariba on the procurement-suite side; iCertis, Cobblestone, Contract Pod AI, Ironclad, Conga on the CLM side), but the CLM specialists trace to the already-lost CLM evaluation, and there is no evidence of an active I&O bake-off. The real current threat is do-nothing: Raymond James keeps running intake manually and told us they are 'not ready' (2026-04-09), so this is a no-decision fight, not a vendor fight. Our wedge is native Merlin agentic intake and orchestration positioned as the opposite of the clunky bolt-on that cost us the CLM deal; the activation is a live Merlin demo to George Jannino and Czarina Whitmore.
sources: SF Task 2026-07-02 (CLM loss to clunky bolt-on AI)

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is negative-to-flat. After Czarina Whitmore's 9 Apr 2026 'not ready for I & O' reply the deal has run entirely on one-way rep nurture (Hackett reports, roundtable and conference invites). The close date was pushed twice on 17 Jun 2026 to a placeholder 15 Jan 2027, and the amount was raised to $400k back in Dec 2025, but no buyer-side step has occurred.

items.change: Buyer declined to progress: Czarina Whitmore emailed 'We're not ready for I & O at this time'
Close date pushed 2026-09-30 to 2027-01-15 (second push same day, from 2026-06-30)
Rep offered to build an RFFS financial-services intake business case for Czarina, cc George
Rep logged CLM-loss debrief: our AI UX was 'clunky and seemed like a bolt-on'

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / no-decision: Raymond James told us on 2026-04-09 they are not ready for Intake & Orchestration and no active evaluation is running, so status-quo manual intake is what we must beat. The ten logged vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, iValua, JAGGAER, Oracle plus the CLM specialists iCertis, Ironclad, Conga, Contract Pod AI, Cobblestone) are carried from the record with no fresh 2026 evidence and no active bake-off; the CLM names relate to the already-lost CLM deal. Our win path is native Merlin agentic intake positioned against the 'clunky bolt-on' perception that lost us CLM.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a no-decision nurture deal on an account that just rejected our CLM bid; the win levers are reframing the AI story, earning one real champion, and manufacturing a compelling event.
flags: Reframe the AI story: the CLM loss debrief called our AI a clunky bolt-on, so lead I&O with native Merlin agentic intake, not a re-skin.
Single-threaded and buyer said 'not ready': earn one real champion (Czarina Whitmore or Alexander Guzman) with a financial-services proof point before any commercials.
No compelling event exists: pair the RFFS-specific intake business case with a dated Raymond James pain so I&O becomes a priority instead of a nurture.

champion_strength.summary: Czarina Whitmore is the only responsive contact and functions as a coach (accepts updates, gives status), but she declined to progress I&O and shows no internal advocacy, so there is no champion. In a BFSI account that just rejected our CLM bid, the top priority is to convert her or Alexander Guzman into a real internal sponsor with a financial-services proof point.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI is under-positioned and carries a scar: we lost the CLM deal because our AI 'was clunky and seemed like a bolt-on' (2026-07-02), and the I&O next steps are framed as workflow, speed and supplier onboarding rather than Merlin agentic AI. The Merlin agentic intake narrative has not been put in front of the buyer.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 4.0 / AIS_Status__c Moderate reflects a workflow-and-transparency framing, not an AI-led one; there are no calls this sweep to test it, but the lost-CLM feedback shows the buyer is open to AI yet unimpressed by a bolt-on experience, so appetite is real but unmet.

items.detail: Primary threat is do-nothing: buyer keeps running intake manually and told us they are not ready, a no-decision risk rather than a loss to a rival.
George Jannino (SVP CPO / economic buyer) has never engaged on this opp; all outreach is one-way.
Reputational drag from the lost CLM deal, where our AI was judged a clunky bolt-on, threatens the I&O AI story unless reframed.
No business case, ROI, or compelling event for I&O; the Hackett benchmark shared was dismissed as a repeat.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling to cold: the last two-way buyer exchange was ~88 days ago and ended in a 'not ready', with only one-way rep nurture since.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A nurture-mode Intake & Orchestration opp on an account where we lost the CLM deal because our AI felt like a clunky bolt-on; Czarina Whitmore said Raymond James isn't ready for I&O (9 Apr 2026), the CPO has never engaged, and this is a no-decision fight, not a competitive one.

win_position.text: This sits low: Raymond James told us 'we're not ready for I & O at this time' (Czarina Whitmore, 9 Apr 2026), the opp is still Qualified with no business case, and the account already rejected our CLM bid over a clunky, bolt-on AI experience.
George Jannino (SVP CPO), the budget owner, has never replied on this opp despite ISM, ProcureCon and Pinnacle invites, so there is no economic-buyer sponsorship.
The close date was pushed twice on 17 Jun 2026 to 15 Jan 2027 with no buyer-driven milestone, signalling a placeholder rather than a real timeline.

deal_momentum.text: The last genuine two-way exchange was Czarina's 9 Apr 2026 'not ready' email; everything since (Hackett reports, event invites) is one-way rep outreach with no reply.
No demo, workshop, or evaluation step has occurred on the I&O opp; engagement is content and event nurture only.

customer_commitment.text: No champion advocates internally; Czarina Whitmore engages politely but declined to progress and shares no internal intelligence.
The buyer has committed to nothing: no next meeting requested, no timeline, and no I&O evaluation criteria on record.

deal_risk.text: The primary risk is a no-decision: Raymond James keeps running intake manually and simply does not buy, not a loss to a named rival.

Reputational drag from the lost CLM deal, where the debrief said our AI 'was clunky and seemed like a bolt-on' (2 Jul 2026), threatens the I&O AI story unless reframed around native Merlin agentic intake.
